Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine and chloride, proteinuria, ketonuria, glucosuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting possible renal impairment and tubular dysfunction. Urine is cloudy, brown, and ammoniacal, raising suspicion for urinary tract pathology. HbA1c is low, indicating no chronic hyperglycemia. Recommend further renal function assessment and evaluation for urinary tract infection or other renal pathology. Clinical correlation advised.
